task_completion.description: Open the bcWebCam settings or options dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam settings/options window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the barcode types or symbologies section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific tab or section within the settings dialog dedicated to selecting barcode types (symbologies) is active and visible.
Checkboxes or toggles for 1D/linear barcodes (such as EAN, UPC, Code 128, Code 39) are visibly unchecked or toggled off.
Checkboxes or toggles for other 2D barcodes (such as DataMatrix, PDF417, Aztec) are visibly unchecked or toggled off.

task_completion.description: Ensure QR Code is the ONLY enabled barcode type
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox or toggle for 'QR Code' is visibly checked/enabled, and it is clearly the only barcode type enabled in the entire list.

task_completion.description: Save the screenshot of the configuration to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog from Snipping Tool, Paint, or another image editor is visible, showing the file being saved as 'qr_only_config.png' specifically in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The authentic bcWebCam application window is visible and being manipulated, rather than a text editor, terminal, or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent used a screenshot tool as requested
integrity.visual_evidence: Snipping Tool, MS Paint (saving a Print Screen), or a similar image capture/saving interface is visible on screen.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot actually depicts the required settings
integrity.visual_evidence: The preview of the image being saved in the screenshot tool clearly shows the bcWebCam barcode settings window, proving the screenshot matches the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is exactly 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\qr_only_config.png'.
